reasoning: As of November 4, 2025, with only 4 races remaining in the season, Racing Bulls is in 6th place with 72 points, while Williams holds 5th place with at least 101 points (their tally from September 21). This creates a massive gap of at least 29 points. In Formula 1, midfield teams typically struggle to score heavily in a single weekend without chaotic circumstances. For Racing Bulls to overturn a nearly 30-point deficit against a strong Williams team (who has Carlos Sainz and Alex Albon and is described as the "most improved team") over just 4 races is highly improbable. Even with sprint weekends, Racing Bulls would need to average around 8-10 points more than Williams per weekend, which contradicts their season average of less than 4 points per race. Expert analyses correctly assess that Williams is heavily favored to secure 5th place. Therefore, the probability of Racing Bulls overcoming this gap is extremely low.
